data.content:
### Restore Parent Module Artifact and Build Child

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/caching-strategies.md

Restores a previously saved parent module artifact from the cache and then builds a child module. This leverages the cached artifact, speeding up the build process for dependent modules.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4

  - uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }}

  - name: Install Dependencies
    if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
    run: ./install.sh
      
  - name: Build
    run: ./build-child-module.sh

  - name: Publish package to public
    run: ./publish.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Elixir Mix dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Elixir Mix project dependencies and build artifacts based on the mix.lock file.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      deps
      _build
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-mix-${{ hashFiles('**/mix.lock') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-mix-
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Carthage dependencies (Swift, Objective-C)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Carthage build artifacts. The cache key is based on the OS and a hash of the Cartfile.resolved file, with a fallback restore key for partial matches.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: Carthage
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-carthage-${{ hashFiles('**/Cartfile.resolved') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-carthage-
```

--------------------------------

### Cache OCaml/Reason Esy dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Esy build artifacts and dependencies for OCaml/Reason projects. It restores the '_export' directory and re-exports dependencies if the cache is missed, using actions/cache@v4.

```yaml
- name: Restore Cache
  id: restore-cache
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: _export
    key:  ${{ runner.os }}-esy-${{ hashFiles('esy.lock/index.json') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-esy-
- name: Esy install
  run: 'esy install'
- name: Import Cache
  run: |
    esy import-dependencies _export
    rm -rf _export

...(Build job)...

# Re-export dependencies if anything has changed or if it is the first time
- name: Setting dependency cache
  run: |
    esy export-dependencies
  if: steps.restore-cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'

```

--------------------------------

### Cache Clojure lein dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches lein project dependencies by targeting the ~/.m2/repository directory and using the project.clj file for cache key generation.

```yaml
- name: Cache lein project dependencies
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.m2/repository
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-clojure-${{ hashFiles('**/project.clj') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-clojure
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Save Parent Module Artifact

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/caching-strategies.md

Builds a parent module and saves its artifact to the cache. This allows the artifact to be reused by child modules in subsequent builds, eliminating redundant compilation.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4

  - name: Build
    run: ./build-parent-module.sh

  - uses: actions/cache/save@v4
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Mint dependencies (Swift)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Mint dependencies, including the lib and bin directories. The cache key is based on the OS and a hash of the Mintfile, with a restore key for partial matches.

```yaml
env:
  MINT_PATH: .mint/lib
  MINT_LINK_PATH: .mint/bin
steps:
  - uses: actions/cache@v4
    with:
      path: .mint
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-mint-${{ hashFiles('**/Mintfile') }}
      restore-keys: |
        ${{ runner.os }}-mint-
```

--------------------------------

### Cache DUB dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches DUB project dependencies for POSIX and Windows systems based on the dub.selections.json file.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.dub
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-dub-${{ hashFiles('**/dub.selections.json') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-dub-
```

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~\AppData\Local\dub
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-dub-${{ hashFiles('**/dub.selections.json') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-dub-
```

--------------------------------

### Cache SBT dependencies (Scala)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache SBT dependencies, including the ivy2 cache and the sbt directory. The cache key is determined by the OS and a hash of the build.sbt file.

```yaml
- name: Cache SBT
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.ivy2/cache
      ~/.sbt
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-sbt-${{ hashFiles('**/build.sbt') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Python pip dependencies (Simple Example)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

A simple example for caching Python pip dependencies on Ubuntu. It uses actions/cache@v4 to cache the default pip cache directory ('~/.cache/pip') based on the OS and a hash of 'requirements.txt'.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.cache/pip
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

```

--------------------------------

### Cache Swift Package Manager build artifacts (Swift)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Swift Package Manager build artifacts in the .build directory. The cache key is generated using the OS and a hash of Package.resolved, with a restore key for flexibility.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: .build
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-spm-${{ hashFiles('**/Package.resolved') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-spm-
```

--------------------------------

### Update Dependency Cache with Licensed

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Updates the cache for third-party dependency licenses using the 'licensed' tool. This command is used when dependencies are added or updated to ensure license compliance.

```bash
licensed cache
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Cargo build artifacts (Rust)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Cargo build artifacts, including the Cargo binary directory, registry index, registry cache, git database, and the target directory. The cache key is based on the OS and a hash of Cargo.lock.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.cargo/bin/
      ~/.cargo/registry/index/
      ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
      ~/.cargo/git/db/
      target/
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Deno dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Deno dependencies for Linux, macOS, and Windows, using the deps.ts file for cache key generation.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.deno
      ~/.cache/deno
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-deno-${{ hashFiles('**/deps.ts') }}
```

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.deno
      ~/Library/Caches/deno
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-deno-${{ hashFiles('**/deps.ts') }}
```

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.deno
      ~\AppData\Local\deno
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-deno-${{ hashFiles('**/deps.ts') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Python pip dependencies (using pip cache dir)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Python pip dependencies by dynamically retrieving the pip cache directory using `pip cache dir` (requires pip 20.1+). It then uses this path with actions/cache@v4, creating a cache key based on OS and a hash of 'requirements.txt', including restore keys.

```yaml
- name: Get pip cache dir
  id: pip-cache
  shell: bash
  run: |
    echo "dir=$(pip cache dir)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

- name: pip cache
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ${{ steps.pip-cache.outputs.dir }}
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

```

--------------------------------

### Cache Bazel build artifacts on Linux

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Bazel build artifacts for Linux using Bazelisk. The cache key incorporates the OS and hashes of relevant Bazel configuration files.

```yaml
- name: Cache Bazel
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.cache/bazel
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bazel-${{ hashFiles('.bazelversion', '.bazelrc', 'WORKSPACE', 'WORKSPACE.bazel', 'MODULE.bazel') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-bazel-
- run: bazelisk test //...
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Erlang Rebar3 dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Erlang Rebar3 dependencies and build output, using OTP_VERSION and rebar.lock for cache key generation.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v2
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.cache/rebar3
      _build
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-erlang-${{ env.OTP_VERSION }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*rebar.lock') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-erlang-${{ env.OTP_VERSION }}-
```

--------------------------------

### Cache PHP Composer dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches PHP dependencies managed by Composer. It first determines the Composer cache directory and then uses actions/cache@v4 to restore it, creating a cache key based on OS and a hash of 'composer.lock'. Restore keys are also defined.

```yaml
- name: Get Composer Cache Directory
  id: composer-cache
  run: |
    echo "dir=$(composer config cache-files-dir)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ${{ steps.composer-cache.outputs.dir }}
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-composer-${{ hashFiles('**/composer.lock') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-composer-

```

--------------------------------

### Skip Install Step if Cache Hit

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/README.md

Shows how to conditionally execute a step, such as installing dependencies, based on the `cache-hit` output of the actions/cache action. This improves workflow efficiency by skipping redundant installations when cached dependencies are available.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4

  - uses: actions/cache@v4
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }}

  - name: Install Dependencies
    if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
    run: /install.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Node.js Yarn dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Node.js dependencies managed by Yarn. It first retrieves the Yarn cache directory path and then uses it with the actions/cache@v4 action, creating a cache key based on OS, 'yarn', and a hash of 'yarn.lock'. It also defines restore keys for incremental caching.

```yaml
- name: Get yarn cache directory path
  id: yarn-cache-dir-path
  run: echo "dir=$(yarn cache dir)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

- uses: actions/cache@v4
  id: yarn-cache # use this to check for `cache-hit` (`steps.yarn-cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'`)
  with:
    path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir-path.outputs.dir }}
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-yarn-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-yarn-

```

--------------------------------

### Cache Pipenv dependencies with actions/cache (Python)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Pipenv virtual environment dependencies. This configuration uses the runner's OS, Python version, and a hash of the Pipfile.lock to create a unique cache key.

```yaml
- name: Set up Python
  id: setup-python
  uses: actions/setup-python@v2

- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.local/share/virtualenvs
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-python-${{ steps.setup-python.outputs.python-version }}-pipenv-${{ hashFiles('Pipfile.lock') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Java Gradle Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Gradle's .gradle/caches and .gradle/wrapper directories. It's recommended to disable Gradle daemons before caching to avoid file locks. The key is based on OS and Gradle build file hashes.

```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.gradle/caches
      ~/.gradle/wrapper
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
    restore-keys: | 
      ${{ runner.os }}-gradle- 

```

--------------------------------

### Restore built artifact from cache

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/restore/README.md

This YAML snippet demonstrates restoring a previously saved build artifact using the `actions/cache/restore@v4` action. It uses the same key and path as the save action, allowing subsequent child module builds to utilize the cached artifact.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4

  - uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }}

  - name: Install Dependencies
    if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
    run: /install.sh

  - name: Build
    run: /build-child-module.sh

  - name: Publish package to public
    run: /publish.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Save Cache Action Usage

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/save/README.md

Demonstrates the basic usage of the actions/cache/save action to cache specified paths with a given key. This is useful for saving artifacts or dependencies generated during a build process.

```yaml
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4

  - name: Install Dependencies
    run: /install.sh

  - name: Build artifacts
    run: /build.sh

  - uses: actions/cache/save@v4
    id: cache
    with:
      path: path/to/dependencies
      key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/lockfiles') }}
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Node.js Lerna dependencies

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Caches Node.js dependencies managed by Lerna using the actions/cache@v4 action. It restores the 'node_modules' directory based on the OS and a hash of the 'yarn.lock' file.

```yaml
- name: restore lerna
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: '**/node_modules'
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}

```

--------------------------------

### Cache Bundler dependencies with ruby/setup-ruby (Ruby)

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache gems with Bundler using the `ruby/setup-ruby` action. It's recommended to use the `bundler-cache: true` option for automatic caching.

```yaml
- uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
  with:
    ruby-version: ...
    bundler-cache: true
```

--------------------------------

### Cache Bazel build artifacts on macOS

Source: https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/examples.md

Cache Bazel build artifacts for macOS using Bazelisk. The cache key is based on the OS and hashes of Bazel configuration files, targeting the temporary directory for macOS builds.

```yaml
- name: Cache Bazel
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      /private/var/tmp/_bazel_runner/
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bazel-${{ hashFiles('.bazelversion', '.bazelrc', 'WORKSPACE', 'WORKSPACE.bazel', 'MODULE.bazel') }}
    restore-keys: |
      ${{ runner.os }}-bazel-
- run: bazelisk test //...
```
